description: Faction sigma-leverage is NON-UNIFORM across the faction stat range (armature A6, computed MC). Bare-stat pool => baseline = stat, so sigma-leverage ~ 1/sqrt(stat): 0.354/pt at stat 2 (+17pct vs 0.30 ref) decaying to 0.189/pt at stat 7 (-37pct, OUT OF BAND low). A point of stat buys decaying leverage as the faction strengthens, compounding the post-F2-ruling punching-up wall (strong rivals hard to act on AND strong-faction stat investment leverage-poor). Distinct from ED-868 (collapse loops) and the artifact-error ED-865/866; this is the underlying attribute-economy property. Aggregating the faction pool (cf. mass-battle ER-9) flattens it. STAGE-4 TESTED: sigma-leverage non-uniformity is NOT fixable by pool aggregation - sigma-leverage ~ 1/sqrt(baseline) is irreducibly non-uniform for any dice pool (flat-agg narrows spread 0.165->0.053 but pushes leverage below band; x2 mult makes low-stat too high, spread 0.233). Only a deterministic+stochastic resolver (constant leverage = slope by construction) fixes it. Strengthens the deterministic+stochastic option on the sigma-leverage dimension specifically. RESOLVER SPEC PRODUCED (domain_action_resolver_spec): tuned deterministic+stochastic candidate P=clamp(0.50+0.10*M, 0.05, 0.90) with graded degree bands; constant 0.10/pt leverage (ED-874 fixed by construction), floor degeneracy 0.069->0.300, punching-up 0.010->0.050, capped ceiling; all Stage-4 checks PASS; Domain Echo OUTPUT unchanged (drop-in). Interface note covers Suppress, Parliamentary Rebuttal, §1.4 Accounting Stability Check (co-design w/ §1.3 recovery, CC-4). Candidate ready for Jordan adopt/tune; NOT applied to canon. Params PROPOSED/untuned. PRECEDENT-BACKED (ners_historical_precedent_matrix entry 2, COIN/structural-contest literature): real political contests are structure-decisive with a stochastic tail, NOT low-variance coin-flips — exactly what deterministic-odds+stochastic-resolution delivers. SECOND justification: the d+s resolver is also what makes the ED-868 faction-collapse bound (Reconstitution) reachable (bare dice leave it near-unreachable at low Influence).

Mass-battle Command sigma-leverage is HIGH at low Command (armature A6, computed): 0.500/pt at Cmd 2 decaying to in-band at Cmd 4-7. Low-Command early-game generals get oversized per-point leverage. Closes attribute_weight_standard GAP #2 (was magnitude-unverified). Same 1/sqrt(baseline) mechanism as ED-874; non-uniform across range.

RESOLVED-APPLIED to canon 2026-05-28 (commit 2327adc4). Trigger-5 firing is NON-MONOTONIC in faction strength because Condition C stacks a pool-size cliff (pool >= 6) onto a magnitude threshold (net <= -2). Computed firing rate by defender pool (recovery-independent MC): Cmd-1 (2D)=0pct [Cond A blocks], Cmd-2 (4D)=1.9pct [needs rare net<=-2], Cmd-3 (6D)=16.6pct [pool>=6 auto-fires C on ANY failure], declining to ~8pct for strong factions. A single Command point (Cmd 2->3) jumps battle->Stability exposure 8.7x. Mid-tier factions are MOST exposed to battle-driven Stability loss; the weakest are shielded. Consider smoothing C (e.g. severity scaled by margin rather than a hard pool>=6 auto-fire). Corrects the earlier hand-traced assumption that weak-faction battles drive a deterministic Stability spiral (CC-2). STRENGTHENED (CC-5): with the canonical Institutional Consolidation recovery, the cliff propagates to COLLAPSE — compound collapse jumps ~25x from Cmd 2 (0.9pct) to Cmd 3 (22.6pct), declining at Cmd 4 (18.6pct), because a fired Trigger-5 denies the deterministic clean-season +1 recovery. A single Command point swinging collapse 25x is a robustness defect -> P1. STAGE-4 TESTED (sim_faction_remediation): Fix-A = remove the pool>=6 clause from Condition C's GATE (keep it in the COST table). Firing curve goes monotonic/flat (~1-2pct, Cmd-3 spike removed) and 100pct of genuine routs (net<=-2) are still punished; only disciplined net 0/-1 large-force withdrawals are spared (matches canon intent). Recommend apply. Independent of the ED-865/874 resolver fork.
jordan_decision: Fix-A applied per Jordan instruction; faction_layer_v30 §0 + Trigger-5 patched; canonical_sources SHA refreshed

description: RESOLVED (was UNREAD, not absent). The faction Stability-recovery mechanic EXISTS: faction_layer_v30 §1.3 Institutional Consolidation = DETERMINISTIC +1 Stability on any Trigger-1-5-free ('clean') season; canon explicitly states it 'replaces the abstract simulator recovery rule.' Conditional paths: peace treaty, recapture territory, Parliamentary Overwhelming, Church Absolution, Lowenritter endorsement; seasonal cap +-2. §1.4 Accounting Stability Check is a cascade-DAMAGE check (fires only on >=2 attr-pts lost), not recovery. Compound sim re-run with this canonical mechanic; SANITY passes; collapse magnitudes now trustworthy. Do NOT file as an open gap.

RE-ASSESSED — NOT a contradiction (corrects my own ED-878 framing). Full read of threadwork_v30 §3.2: L622 base table ('FR Lock/Dissolution any scale: -1 additional; min total -2') and L631-633 PP-196 ('Object/Personal: -1 total [scale 0 + FR -1]; Relational: -2 total [-1 scale + -1 FR]; FR surcharge cap-EXEMPT') describe ONE coherent rule across scales, not two competing values. The 'min total -2' holds at Relational+ where FR is typically used; PP-196 clarifies the smaller-scale totals and the cap-exemption. Independently corroborated at L177 (fieldwork note cites 'FR/Binding cap-exempt per PP-196' as established). PP-196 is a consistent elaboration, not a divergence. No patch needed; do not file as a contradiction. (CC-4 lesson: read fully before asserting a defect.)
jordan_decision: n/a — false contradiction, resolved by full read

description: Combat secondary consistency items (consolidated, C3/C5/C6). C3: out-of-bounds x Pool-Floor-5 interaction underspecified (does the floor apply when a combatant is pushed OOB?). C5: Health super-linear at low Endurance via (End+6)(MW+1) MW step (now also captured quantitatively in ED-826 + the I-1 win-rate refinement below). C6: Strength damage-multiplier vs Power terminology ambiguity. Combat is mechanically sound (NERS PASS); these are clarity/consistency, not balance. RE-ASSESSED: not evidence-patchable. C6 'Strength multiplier' appears only in Garrison Strength (settlement-defense formula, unrelated to the personal-combat Strength stat); personal combat uses clear floor(STR/2) terms — no live terminology conflict found. C3 out-of-bounds x Pool-Floor: no defined OOB mechanic surfaces in combat_v30 — it is underspecified-by-ABSENCE, and specifying what OOB should do is owner-intent (design), not an evidence patch. C5 already captured in ED-826. Combat is NERS-PASS; these are low-priority clarity items requiring Jordan's design intent, retained as such.
jordan_decision: pending — C3/C6 need owner design intent (clarity, low priority); C5 -> ED-826

description: RESOLVED-APPLIED 2026-05-29. faction_layer §Varfell and params/bg/core asserted 'Varfell expansion is purely military / does not convert populations ideologically' — the rationale for the 2026-04-19 Cultural REFORMATION strike (VTM-dependent action; VTM was a placeholder, validly struck). But the Conviction-Track redesign (conviction_track_v30) reintroduced the ideological-conversion capability VTM-FREE as Cultural RECLAMATION (Varfell: Influence vs Ob 2, target-territory Piety -1 toward Einhir Restoration pole). The stale 'purely military' prose was never reconciled. Per Jordan 2026-05-29: Varfell takes ALL universal faction actions and has Cultural Reclamation as a non-military Piety-ALIGNMENT vector (NOT a control transfer, NOT a victory path). Reconciled the prose in faction_layer_v30 §Varfell + params/bg/core (L5 comment + L226 row) toward the live mechanic; Reformation + VTM remain struck. SOLE victory condition remains Peninsular Sovereignty (GD-1) — no faction-specific/religious/non-military victory path. Documentation reconciliation; no mechanical change (Cultural Reclamation was already live).
affected_docs: canon/supersession_register.yaml (CR-STRIKE/VTM entries still carry the historical 'purely military' rationale — flagged for editorial owner to add a forward-pointer to ED-880; not edited here to preserve the audit trail)
source: Jordan flag 2026-05-29; repo investigation (conviction_track_v30 Cultural Reclamation)
jordan_decision: reconcile toward live Cultural Reclamation; Reformation/VTM stay struck; Piety-alignment only (no control, no victory); peninsula sole victory (GD-1)

description: GD-3 (Revolt -> Insurgency -> Faction) has NO down-path: the pipeline can escalate but cannot DISSOLVE. Per ners_historical_precedent_matrix entry 4 (RAND, How Insurgencies End, 89 cases): insurgent defeat is the MODAL outcome; loss of sanctuary/sponsorship is the strongest predictor of defeat; ~10yr average duration. GD-3 currently cannot represent the most common real outcome -> NERS R-FAIL on the missing down-path. Recommend owner-authored multi-path dissolution: military defeat / sponsor-withdrawal / amnesty-negotiated / else-persist, with sponsor-withdrawal the high-value add. Surfaced by the precedent reconciliation, not by this session's faction-collapse work (collapse itself is bounded — ED-868). RESOLVED-CANDIDATE (insurgency_dissolution_proposal): tested 4-path down-path (military defeat / sponsor withdrawal / amnesty / persist) subsuming pipeline Options A/B as the military exit + adding the RAND-dominant sponsor path. 8k MC: defeat MODAL (0.97), sponsor-loss the dominant defeat driver tracking P(sponsor-loss) per RAND, amnesty a minority exit, stalemate reachable; Stage-4 found+fixed 2 defects (sponsor misattribution, amnesty over-fire). Completes pipeline forward-flags INSURGENCY-DISSOLUTION-001 + INSURGENCY-PROMOTED-DISSOLUTION-001. NOT written to canon (GD-3 §B requires Jordan ratification). Amnesty path uses the d+s resolver (ED-865/874 cross-dep).
jordan_decision: pending ratification — adopt/tune the 4-path mechanic + P(sponsor-loss) knob
